title: Daily Briefing — Jun 03
headline: Tech still leads but financials coughing up a lung — watch the canary.
body: Right, so today's tape is mixed: SPY holding well above its 20 MA ($744.92) at $756.05, but under the hood, tech momentum is slowing and financials (XLF) are breaking support. VIX is low at 15.77 — feels calm, but that's usually when you want to sharpen your risk radar.

Tech (XLK) remains the only sector with LOW risk and leading status (RS=127.6; price at $196.89 well above all MAs). Meanwhile, lagging sectors like XLF ($50.55, triggered bear, below all MAs) and XLC (exit) are flashing caution. Rotation favours select healthcare and industrials — XLV, XLI, XLB are improving, but nothing turbo. Energy (XLE) is still high risk and lagging.

Financials breaking their 20 MA and triggering bear (R010 canary rule) is a major warning — economy sickness alert. Sentiment is in moderate greed (57), not extreme, but margin debt and high retail flows have been near record, so I'm not chasing. If it's in the press, it's in the price. Less trades = more wins.
callout_body:
SPY 20 MA: $744.92 (holds, bullish)
XLF: $50.55 (triggered bear, below 51.42 MA)
NVDA: $218.97 20 MA — watch for daily close below for exit
XLK: $196.89 (leading, safe haven zone)
VIX: 15.77 (complacency zone — stay tighten-up)
XLE, XLF, XLC, XLRE: avoid, lagging/high risk
Tech: Only sector to overweight short-term
action_items: Watch NVDA — if daily close < $218.97, exit any longs (R007 technical rule)
Rotate to XLK/XLV/XLI (tech, healthcare, industrials) only if holding above their weekly 20 MA — hands off lagging sectors
closing_quote: If bonds freak out, everything breaks. Right now? The canary’s wheezing, so don’t get greedy.
